title: Agentic Life Infrastructure Market Registry
description: A directional, evidence-linked market map for systems that may help context compound, specialization compose, sovereignty remain real, quality become verifiable, and multiple life domains share one substrate.

methodology.warning: Scores are FrankX editorial assessments based on linked public material. They are not laboratory benchmarks, security certifications, legal advice, or procurement recommendations.

axes.context: Context can persist and improve across tools or sessions.
axes.composition: Agents, skills, protocols, and workflows can compose through explicit contracts.
axes.sovereignty: Users can export, rehost, audit, or self-host meaningful system state.
axes.verifiability: The system exposes traces, tests, evaluations, or machine-checkable receipts.
axes.multiDomain: The system can support more than a single narrow feature or workflow.

strategicRoles.build: FrankX/Starlight systems we actively own and evolve.
strategicRoles.integrate: High-value components to place behind stable adapters.
strategicRoles.partner: Organizations or standards with credible ecosystem alignment.
strategicRoles.compete: Products that overlap the Agentic Life Infrastructure thesis.
strategicRoles.inspire: Architectures worth learning from without adopting wholesale.
strategicRoles.watch: Relevant systems that need more evidence or a clearer fit.

systems.name: Agentic Operating System Standard
systems.summary: Portable contracts for modules, agents, skills, workflows, loops, ledgers, gates, adapters, and profiles.
systems.nextAction: Stabilize the public conformance profile and adapter contract.
systems.risk: A standard without conformance tests can become documentation theater.

systems.name: Agentic Architecture Field Guide
systems.summary: Public implementation reference for composing agent runtimes, memory, policy boundaries, evaluation, and domain modules.
systems.nextAction: Use the guide as the public architecture baseline and attach executable conformance receipts to each pattern.
systems.risk: A reference architecture can look complete before its contracts are proven against production workloads.

systems.name: Starlight Memory
systems.summary: Sovereign local_core memory authority with replaceable provider adapters and provenance-aware atoms.
systems.nextAction: Ship adapter conformance tests for Mem0, Graphiti, and file-native storage.
systems.risk: Multiple writers require conflict policy and actor-aware provenance.

systems.name: Starlight Evals
systems.summary: Receipt-first evaluation substrate for private estate tasks, model arenas, and maker-not-equal-checker verification.
systems.nextAction: Add shared receipt schema, baseline suites, and CI adapters for all life modules.
systems.risk: Estate-specific suites can overfit unless public and adversarial tasks remain in the mix.

systems.name: Hermes Agent
systems.summary: Extensible agent harness with tools, skills, memory, scheduling, projects, and local execution surfaces.
systems.nextAction: Use as a replaceable control plane while keeping life state in local_core.
systems.risk: Harness memory and schedules must not silently become the only system of record.

systems.name: Model Context Protocol
systems.summary: Open protocol for exposing tools, resources, and prompts to AI applications through stable server contracts.
systems.nextAction: Standardize life-module tools and resources without placing authority in any one client.
systems.risk: Protocol interoperability does not automatically provide identity, policy, memory, or evals.

systems.name: Agent2Agent Protocol
systems.summary: Interoperability protocol for discovery and collaboration between independently built agents.
systems.nextAction: Prototype a bounded A2A handoff with receipts before making it a fleet default.
systems.risk: Agent interoperability can increase attack surface and authority ambiguity.

systems.name: Mem0
systems.summary: General-purpose memory layer for agents with managed and open-source deployment paths.
systems.nextAction: Benchmark as a derived adapter against local_core recall and deletion contracts.
systems.risk: Managed and open-source capabilities differ; provider IDs must not become authoritative.

systems.name: Graphiti
systems.summary: Open temporal knowledge graph engine for agent memory and changing entity relationships.
systems.nextAction: Test temporal recall where entity history justifies graph complexity.
systems.risk: Operational complexity may not pay for simple preference or episodic memory.

systems.name: Letta
systems.summary: Stateful agent runtime built around self-editing memory blocks and long-lived agents.
systems.nextAction: Study memory-block ergonomics and compare against atom-plus-adapter authority.
systems.risk: Long-lived agent state can couple memory authority too tightly to one runtime.

systems.name: LangGraph
systems.summary: Graph-based durable orchestration for stateful, human-in-the-loop agent workflows.
systems.nextAction: Use for durable graph workflows where replay and interrupts justify framework weight.
systems.risk: Framework state is not a substitute for portable life memory or cross-harness identity.

systems.name: OpenAI Agents SDK
systems.summary: Provider SDK for agents, handoffs, guardrails, sessions, and tracing.
systems.nextAction: Keep behind provider adapters; test handoff and trace export against local receipts.
systems.risk: Model and hosted-service coupling can weaken portability.

systems.name: Google Agent Development Kit
systems.summary: Open agent framework with multi-agent composition, tools, sessions, evaluation, and deployment integrations.
systems.nextAction: Evaluate as an alternate orchestration adapter and A2A reference implementation.
systems.risk: Cloud deployment convenience can obscure where durable state and traces become authoritative.

systems.name: CrewAI
systems.summary: Role-oriented multi-agent framework and enterprise automation platform.
systems.nextAction: Track enterprise control and eval depth; avoid role-play orchestration without evidence contracts.
systems.risk: Role labels can create multi-agent theater when scopes, stop conditions, and receipts are weak.

systems.name: AG2
systems.summary: Open multi-agent conversation and workflow framework derived from the AutoGen ecosystem.
systems.nextAction: Monitor evolution and compare handoff semantics with LangGraph and ADK.
systems.risk: Conversational agent loops can be token-heavy and difficult to bound.

systems.name: OpenFang
systems.summary: Open-source agent operating system in Rust with local-first runtime ambitions.
systems.nextAction: Study runtime packaging and installation; differentiate with life modules, portable memory, and receipts.
systems.risk: Agent OS positioning is broader than proven multi-domain user outcomes.

systems.name: n8n
systems.summary: Workflow automation platform with self-hosting, integrations, and AI workflow nodes.
systems.nextAction: Use as automation fabric for reversible connectors, not as the life-state authority.
systems.risk: Workflow JSON can become hidden business logic without tests and ownership boundaries.

systems.name: Braintrust
systems.summary: Evaluation and observability platform built around datasets, experiments, scorers, and production traces.
systems.license: See SDK repository and service terms
systems.nextAction: Benchmark CI ergonomics and export receipts against Starlight Evals.
systems.risk: Hosted eval data needs explicit privacy-class routing.

systems.name: Arize Phoenix
systems.summary: Source-available AI observability and evaluation platform with OpenTelemetry and OpenInference support.
systems.nextAction: Prototype local OpenTelemetry traces for agent trajectories and memory operations.
systems.risk: Observability volume and sensitive spans require retention and redaction controls.

systems.name: Langfuse
systems.summary: Open-source LLM engineering platform for traces, prompt management, datasets, and evaluations.
systems.license: MIT core with commercial components; verify current repository
systems.nextAction: Compare local deployment cost and export fidelity with Phoenix.
systems.risk: Self-hosting still requires governance for PII, secrets, and retention.

systems.name: LangSmith
systems.summary: Managed tracing, evaluation, deployment, and observability tightly integrated with LangChain and LangGraph.
systems.nextAction: Use only where LangGraph trace depth outweighs sovereignty and stack coupling.
systems.risk: Tight ecosystem coupling and hosted traces can reduce portability.

systems.name: DeepEval
systems.summary: Open-source, test-oriented framework for LLM and agent evaluation.
systems.nextAction: Adapt deterministic life-infrastructure contracts into a pytest-style suite.
systems.risk: LLM judges still require calibration and cannot replace hard policy checks.

systems.name: Ragas
systems.summary: Open-source evaluation framework focused on retrieval, generation, and agent quality.
systems.nextAction: Use for retrieval quality while keeping policy and trajectory checks separate.
systems.risk: RAG metrics cover only part of a multi-tool agent trajectory.

systems.name: MLflow GenAI
systems.summary: Open platform extending experiment tracking, tracing, evaluation, and model lifecycle management into GenAI applications.
systems.nextAction: Evaluate as a durable experiment ledger where existing MLflow infrastructure exists.
systems.risk: Platform breadth can add operational weight for small local-first teams.

systems.name: Supermemory
systems.summary: Memory and context infrastructure for AI applications with managed developer APIs.
systems.nextAction: Track API and self-host maturity; compare recall against Mem0 and local_core.
systems.risk: Managed context convenience can create another non-portable authority layer.

systems.name: Cognee
systems.summary: Open data and memory engine that turns information into graph-oriented AI memory.
systems.nextAction: Compare graph construction, provenance, and deletion semantics with Graphiti.
systems.risk: Graph ingestion quality and deletion semantics need workload-specific verification.

systems.name: Claude Code
systems.summary: Terminal coding harness with tools, hooks, subagents, skills, MCP, and repository-native instructions.
systems.nextAction: Treat as a replaceable execution harness fed by repo contracts and sovereign memory.
systems.risk: Harness-local context does not compound automatically across other tools.

systems.name: OpenAI Codex
systems.summary: Coding agent surfaces for repository work, cloud tasks, and terminal execution.
systems.nextAction: Keep git and receipt contracts as the coordination plane across Codex and other harnesses.
systems.risk: Cloud task context and local session context can fragment without shared contracts.

systems.name: Cursor
systems.summary: AI-native code editor with repository context, rules, agents, and background execution.
systems.nextAction: Track rules, background-agent receipts, and portability across editor and external harnesses.
systems.risk: Editor-native memory and rules can become another isolated context island.
